notes/2026-01-30-02.html.description: I’ve been wanting to make some sort of digital garden page for a while but never got around to it. For some reason, today, I just kinda feel like doing it. No idea what’s gonna go here. It’s probably just gonna become a glorified blog, to be honest.I think I should wander around the internet looking at other digital gardens a little more.

notes/2026-02-01.html.description:
[ sunday ] ⚬ [ feb. 1st, 2026 ]Man, do I wish I was more into BG3, because that fandom is eating well. I can’t remember ever being all too hyped for it when it first came out and I think I got like… immediate hype fatigue. Or maybe even reactive hype fatigue (except within the first week of release. So… unfairly expedited hype fatigue, maybe.) It’s definitely not a bad game, I actually have a lot of respect for it, I think I just never clicked with it. I’m hoping that with this playthrough, maybe I’ll finally find something to love about it, but it’s one of those things where I looked at all of the primary characters and just didn’t feel a pull to any of them. They’re not bad in any way, I just never really got that spark of, “Oh my god, I love this little bitch!” that I often feel moments before finding a new fictional special interest.
So this time around, I’m playing it heavily modded just to go through the storyline and finally get to know the characters and see if I wind up liking them by the end.I’ve got them essentially cosplaying my OCs for a little extra dopamine to keep me motivated. Shadowheart is currently cosplaying my OC, Krist, and I low-key love it, though I feel like when I find Karlach, I’m going to swap the Krist cosplay over to her. From what I’ve seen of Karlach, I feel like she and Krist have the most similar personalities.I turned Tav into Evangeline and Astarion into her twin brother, August. I went into this knowing that Astarion is a bit sassy by nature and figured that’d align well with August, who is also an aristocratic little shit. But holy fck, I did not expect Astarion to be that catty. It’s almost too much to see him cosplaying August. One of my friends once suggested that Gortash might be a better fit and I’m low-key thinking maybe I might swap them later, we’ll see.Last night I played up to the point where they recruit Gale. So far, my party is just Tav, Astarion, Shadowheart, and Gale. Shadowheart is kind of my favorite so far, she just feels the most like a vibe. But I’m also low-key getting tsundere or even yandere vibes. I don’t know much about her storyline yet, I’ve seen spoilers but didn’t care enough to commit them to memory so it’s a spoiler semi-free playthrough, somehow.I think at one point, someone told me the entire Astarion storyline but it really went in one ear and out the other, rip.
Something-something, vampire, his boss sings a musical bit, homeboy’s avoidant-attachment as heck due to trauma from said musical boss guy, at some point he can get zapped by Dr. Evil’s solar powered laser beam, and then “Ascended” Astarion or something.I don’t know what that means, but okay.I’m sure it’ll make sense at some point.Gale so far is… I’m on the fence, like, he’s mostly entertaining but then he’ll say something that leaves me cringing but maybe that’s part of his appeal, he’s like just one of those people who have high natural charisma but really low wisdom, but conversationally. (I feel you, bro.)

Maybe I’ll periodically blog about my BG3 thoughts.It’s actually been such a long time since I played a game that wasn’t open-ended. Project Zomboid has been my jam for the better part of the last three years, maybe four? Nightingale and Enshrouded were pretty big time blocks as well, both are also open-ended and not very story-driven. I actually can’t remember the last game I played that had an actual storyline going on. Maybe the campaign mode in Anno 1800.So yeah… that’s a thing that happened.

headers.heading: 𝚏𝚒𝚗𝚊𝚕𝚕𝚢 𝚙𝚕𝚊𝚢𝚒𝚗𝚐 𝚋𝚊𝚕𝚍𝚞𝚛'𝚜 𝚐𝚊𝚝𝚎 𝟹 [ 𝘫𝘶𝘴𝘵 𝘵𝘳𝘺𝘪𝘯𝘨 𝘵𝘰 𝘷𝘪𝘣𝘦 𝘵𝘣𝘩 ]

notes/2026-02-01-02.html.description: While writing that entry about BG3, I was trying to think of the word for that feeling I got and when looking into it, learned about “psychological reactance” and felt like writing it down. Ultimately, this isn’t the thing that I was feeling in my disinterest in BG3 (which was just hype fatigue.) Though it was still kind of an interesting topic.Psychological reactance is essentially when you feel like you’re being told what to like, or aggressively influenced and so you get this knee-jerk, negative emotional response as a result.Kind of neat, I guess, that there’s a word for the feeling.Another term I came across was “overexposure effect” or “overexposure backlash” where when you can’t escape something that’s crazy popular, the repeated exposure starts to feel grating. I definitely think that I’ve felt this for other crazy popular media that felt inescapable in online fandom spaces, though I can’t remember any off the top of my head at the moment (if it were memorable to me, I’d remember it.)One of the things I’ve been trying to check myself on in the last few years is unprompted negativity and contrarian behaviors. (I have a lot more to say about that topic and my feels about it, but I’ll save that for another entry.)So part of this is that I really want to give things a fair chance and not just default to hating things because they’re inescapable.Unplugging from social media has really helped with this, I think—if I’m not in those populated fandom spaces, I don’t get bombarded with the overexposure.Which like… damn, that’s also a whole topic that one could make a write-up about, too. How many genuinely good movies, games or books have I skipped out on because the overexposure and hype fatigue had me hating on it?I guess it’s just another reason why I’m so done with social media platforms these days and would rather just… idk, hang out in a more individual, closed space like this with only a handful of friends’ neocities pages to check out every now and then.The less I see how massive a fandom is, the less likely I am to feel hype fatigue or even that “reactance” feeling.I just keep thinking about how at the end of it all, I don’t want to look back on my life and see how much I deprived myself of fond memories and experiences solely because I was stuck in a negative or contrarian-adjacent mindset.The last time I went to an anime convention, I saw a lot of fandoms where the booths in the Artists Alley were doing punch cards. It was actually such a wholesome, community-operated little thing and I thought it was so cool to see in action, even if I was really not into any of the fandoms that were doing it. Luckily, I found one set of people doing it for Final Fantasy XIV and had a blast collecting as many punch card stickers as I could.But like… imagine how much more fun I could have had if I were even just a casual enjoyer of the other fandoms doing it?Then there’s how much fanart and fanworks that get made by the sheer armies of fans in those large fandoms. I always say that BG3 fans are “eating good” because there’s no shortage of fanworks to enjoy if you’re into it.I think I’d like my default reaction to most things be closer to neutral/positive, even if I only ever become a casual or outskirts enjoyer of any huge media. I dunno, man, it just feels like a more pleasant space to be in.

notes/2026-02-23.html.description:
“Loop Stitch Binding Staples” & “Loop Staple Binding”
These are so funky looking… just funky lil dudes on a saddlestitch?I’ve never seen these before. What the heck they’re kind of cool?What they’re for: Bound pamphlets can be gathered into binders made specially for loop staples. The idea is that you can put them in (usually instructional or tech manuals) and them add later editions into the same binder as they become available. There are larger versions for bigger binds (like maps) that can be hung sort of like hangers? Apparently? These are done with what’s called a “Saddle Hook Stapler” I don’t know why this is so neat to me. I guess because I’ve never seen it before. It’s kind of satisfying to look at, I guess.

notes/2026-02-25.html.description:
Head Over Heels / ヘッド・オーヴァー・ヒールズ（Tears For Fears / ティアーズ・フォー・フィアーズ）1985 : 洋楽和訳 Neverending Musicティアーズ・フォー・フィアーズは"Everybody Wants To Rule The World"も"Shout"もサウンド的には一度聴いたらなかなか頭のなかから離れないような病みつき曲だったのですが、歌詞的にはちょっと難解で、深くホレ込むようなことはありませんでした。（このブログでこれまneverendingmusic.blog.jpJust thought this was cool—went searching for the Japanese-language “Tears for Fears” fandom and a Japanese translation of “Head Over Heels” because I’ve enjoyed that song so much recently. Found a neato blog where the author translates the lyrics into Japanese and goes into the nuances behind some of the chosen words for their nostalgia factor.I’m always so fascinated by the words that are chosen when someone translates prose, poetry, or lyrics into different languages.Something that I don’t think people are always aware of us how some words have so much emotional weight and connotations that don’t always occur when the phrase gets translated to another language.
Even the phrase “Head Over Heels” which does not seem to have an equivalent in Japanese, is analyzed a little deeper in another article by the same author, where they elaborate on the meaning. (This one explores it in reference to the song by “The Go-Go’s” who also had a song with that phrase for the title.)In a reverse of this entire concept of phrases that don’t have exact equivalents, this all reminds me of my favorite Japanese phrase, “恋の予感” or, “koi no yokan” which I’ve seen a couple translations of.Some people translate it as a “premonition of love” but I think I favor “the inevitability of love” because premonition and inevitability have different nuances that change the impact. In English, while we have the phrase “love at first sight” it just isn’t the same—there’s something uhh… sucrose-flavored about it, but maybe the flavor’s just tainted by capitalism’s handling of the word.I think that there’s something far heavier about “koi no yokan” as more an inevitability of love that just tickles my cosmic horror-loving brain more, I guess.
Speaking of which, have you ever considered love as a form of cosmic horror?The tl;dr itinerary of this note, courtesy of mixed amphetamine salts:“Head Over Heels” → Interest in the translation of a phrase without exact equivalent → Example of a phrase without exact equivalent (恋の予感) → Words have unique and nuanced meanings despite similarities in use-case scenarios → “koi no yokan” is not like “love at first sight” because it is intrinsically a bit darker and more existential → Love as a form of cosmic (existential) horror is a pretty rad concept, by the way.

notes/2026-02-27.html.description: I really want to make a neocities at some point but I feel like… it would just be redundant for my workflow (just my own workflow, not dragging neocities at all, I love it.) I just feel like I get what I want done with this site/garden just fine (although I do wish it were self-hosted or indie web as opposed to corporate-owned… capitalist dystopia be like.)Today I think I got kinda close to attempting to wrap my head around how an obsidian.md + 11ty + github + maybe neocities stack would look but I am really not a coder at all and got as far as building a github repository before the adderall wore off. Oh well.I think my smooth brain just needs things as WYSIWYG as possible when it comes to digital gardens and web stuff. I need as little friction as possible.That said, I am still going to very happily appreciate neocities sites and gardens from a distance.They really are such a lovely refusal of the dead internet theory. ♡I found the happy middle ground between using notion and neocities, best of both worlds.

notes/2026-03-03-02.html.description: banji got me into this… digital pomodoro timer game called on together and im actually enjoying it so damb much :purple-sparkles:it’s basically just this lil thing i can have running in the background and let my character do their focus tasks and get points for completing pomodoro timed stuff. points turn around and get put into costumes and furnishings. so far i haven’t seen any cash shop content which i appreciate so much.the music’s not so bad either. reminds me of the stardew valley soundtrack in a way? most tracks are a little more chill-hop but there are a few that really get me feeling like i left SDV going in the background while I’m cleaning or doing other work.

notes/2026-03-04.html.description: wren: quick zhen draw a dick on their face

notes/2026-03-06.html.description: Sigh.I want to love Obsidian so bad.I’m tired, Grandpa.

notes/2026-03-10.html.description: One day I might talk about my OCs in public. For now, just have some really out of context photo mode screenshots of them enjoying a nice afternoon while their pain daddy priest struggles not to slip on mossy rocks in the background.

headers.heading: 𝒕𝒉𝒊𝒔 𝒊𝒔 𝒂 𝒗𝒆𝒓𝒚 𝒔𝒆𝒓𝒊𝒐𝒖𝒔 𝒔𝒕𝒐𝒓𝒚. [ 𝘢𝘣𝘥𝘪𝘳𝘢𝘬 𝘧𝘢𝘤𝘦𝘱𝘭𝘢𝘯𝘵𝘴 𝘪𝘯 𝘵𝘩𝘦 𝘣𝘢𝘤𝘬𝘨𝘳𝘰𝘶𝘯𝘥 ]

info_showCustomIcons.description: Show custom icons for files and folders

info_showDefaultFolderIcons.description: Show a default icon of a folder for every folder in the tree

info_showDefaultFileIcons.description: Show a default icon of a file for every file in the tree

info_defaultFolderIcon.description: The icon to use for folders. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultFileIcon.description: The icon to use for files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: The icon to use for media files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_exposeStartingPath.description: Whether or not to show the current file in the file tree when the page is first loaded

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_minCollapseDepth.description: Only allow outline items to be collapsed if they are at least this many levels deep in the tree.

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_displayTitle.description: Descriptive title to show above the feature

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site
